data.description:
## Finding (S1 — verified)
The module-size CI gate fully EXEMPTS allowlisted files, so a grandfathered file can grow without bound. Evidence: `attest.py` 268->837 (+212%), `passes.py` 589->839, `det_floor.py` 733->849, `inbound_differ.py` 754->802 all grew AFTER being allowlisted.

Citations: `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` (bare filenames); gate in `.github/workflows/test.yml` (`awk '$1>800'` then `comm -23` vs allowlist).

## Remediation
Convert the allowlist from bare filenames to `path <max-lines>` rows (ceiling = current LOC at grandfathering). The gate compares each allowlisted file against ITS OWN recorded ceiling; exceeding it fails CI ("reduce it or bump the row — a reviewed act"). New non-allowlisted files keep the 800 rule. Also drop the `__init__.py` filename-pattern exemption (give it a pinned ceiling row instead) so it is gate-visible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Allowlist rows carry a per-file LOC ceiling
- [ ] test.yml fails when an allowlisted file grows past its ceiling; shrinking is free
- [ ] `__init__.py` is gate-visible (pinned row, not filename-exempt)
- [ ] Current allowlisted files pass at their pinned ceilings
data.title: Add a per-file growth ratchet to the module-size allowlist
